summary.quantification_method: Internal standard quantification using stable isotope-labeled (SIL) peptides

primary_variables.matched_definition: Permanent loss of function through full gene knockout (for example CRISPR KO or deletion).
evidence: the genes encoding m6A eraser proteins (i.e., ALKBH5, FTO) and the catalytic subunit of the major m6A writer complex (i.e., METTL3) being individually ablated
isogenic cells with the catalytic subunit of the m6A writer complex (i.e., METTL3) and m6A eraser proteins (i.e., ALKBH5 and FTO) being genetically ablated
we previously knocked out ALKBH5, FTO, and METTL3 genes in this cell line
ALKBH5−/−, FTO−/−, and METTL3−/− cells relative to parental HEK293T cells
METTL3−/− cells relative to parental HEK293T cells
ALKBH5−/− cells relative to parental HEK293T cells
FTO−/− cells relative to parental HEK293T cells

replicate_hints: Three replicate samples were initially prepared
only two replicates of METTL3-/- cell samples were analyzed
averaged from the quantification results of 2-3 biological replicates
multiplex_channel_hints: incorporating stable isotope-labeled (SIL) peptides as internal or surrogate standards

evidence: relative expression levels of 117, 119, and 118 RWE proteins in ALKBH5, FTO, and METTL3 knockout cells with respect to parental HEK293T cells
relative expression levels of 117, 119, and 118 RWE proteins in ALKBH5, FTO, and METTL3 knockout cells with respect to parental HEK293T cells
relative expression levels of 117, 119, and 118 RWE proteins in ALKBH5, FTO, and METTL3 knockout cells with respect to parental HEK293T cells

quantification_strategy: Internal standard quantification using stable isotope-labeled (SIL) peptides

base_candidates_ranked.reason: While the experiment is LC-PRM (targeted), the use of spiked-in SIL peptides for relative quantification mimics the heavy/light ratio logic of isobaric/SILAC workflows. No standard DDA LFQ or DIA workflow fits the targeted nature.

modifier_candidates_ranked.reason: The experiment explicitly uses LC-PRM (Parallel Reaction Monitoring) with a predefined library of RWE proteins.
